description: The Camera component represents a Unity Camera which is centered on the slot and facing in the forward direction.

fields.DoubleBuffered: Determines if DoubleBuffering is applied or not.
fields.ForwardOnly: Determine whether the render technique is only forward. This can badly effect performance.
fields.Projection: Determines whether it's perspective or orthographic
fields.OrthographicSize: The size of the render-output in orthographic view. (Only active when ``Projection`` is set to ``Orthographic``
fields.FieldOfView: The vertical field of view of the camera. (Only active when ``Projection`` is set to ``Perspective``
fields.NearClipping: The point in meters where the camera ignores near objects.
fields.FarClipping: The point in meters where the camera ignores far objects.
fields.UseTransformScale: Determines if the scale of the camera should be taken into account.
fields.Clear: See Camera Clear Mode for what this does.
fields.Viewport: 2D rectangular where the camera is allowed to render
fields.Depth: Whether this camera should be rendered before or after other cameras render.
fields.RenderTexture: The RenderTextureProvider input in order to get a ITexture2D
fields.Postprocessing: Determines if post processing is allowed.
fields.MotionBlur: Determines if MotionBlur is rendered or not.
fields.RenderShadows: Determines if shadows are rendered or not.
fields.SelectiveRender: A list of SyncReferences to slots the camera is allowed to render.
fields.ExcludeRender: A list of SyncReferences to slots the camera is NOT allowed to render.

usage: Put the component onto a slot, and position the slot. The produced image can either be accessed through a RenderTextureProvider component or by creating a Texture2D asset using the Render To Texture Asset Protoflux node.

notes.title: See also
notes.text: * CameraPortal, which is often used as a mirror.
